titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 616

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=57 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-06 15:19:33.995842+00 | translation moa=60 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-06 15:19:33.971737+00

p.cl: Alexander dei Gratia Rex Scottorum . Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue . Clericis et laicis  Salutem.

Sciant presentes et futuri me concessisse . ⁊
hac Carta mea confirmasse Roberto . de Aubein’ . ⁊ heredibus suis . illas uiginti marcas que mihi debentur annuatim de waitinga quam
Prior ⁊ Conuentus de Coldingham’ . mihi debent de terris suis de Coldingham’ . donec Ego uel heredes mei eidem Roberto . uel here
dibus suis dederimus . uiginti marcatas terre ex australi parte Maris Scocie . uel inter le Muneth . ⁊ Mare Scocie. Quare volo . ut
predictus Robertus . de Aubein’ . ⁊ heredes sui illas uiginti marcas percipiant singulis annis ad pascha. Et precipio Priori de
Coldingham’ . ⁊ Balliuis eius . quatinus annuatim sine impedimento illas uiginti marcas eis reddant ad pascha . donec Ego uel
heredes mei eis dederimus predictas uiginti marcatas terre. Sicut Carta domini Regis . Willelmi patris mei inde facta predicto Roberto .
testatur.

Testibus . Willelmo de Boscho . Cancellario meo . Willelmo de Valoniis . Camerario meo . Philippo . de Mubray . Galfrido . filio . Ricardi . vicecomite . de Pert’ .
Philippo . de Lundin’.
Alexander, by the Grace of God King of the Scots, To all worthy men of his whole land, Clerk and lay : Greeting.

Let present and future know that I have granted, and by this my charter made firm to Robert de Aubein, and to his heirs, those twenty merks which is owed annually as waiting which the Prior and Convent of Coldingham owe to me for their lands at Coldingham, until I or my heirs shall have given to Robert, or to his heirs, twenty merks’ worth of land on the south side of the Sea of Scotia, or between the Mounth and the Sea of Scotia. Wherefore I will, that the aforesaid Robert de Aubein, and his heirs, should receive those twenty merks each year at Easter. And I command the Prior of Coldingham, and his Baillies, that they should pay those twenty merks to them at Easter, without hindrance until I or my heirs shall have given to them the aforesaid twenty merks’ worth of land, just as the Charter of the lord King, William my father, produced on the matter for the aforesaid Robert, bears witness.
As witnesses : William del Bois, my Chancellor; William de Valognes, my Chamberlain; Philip de Mowbray; Geoffrey son of Richard, sheriff of Perth; Philip of Lundin.
At Perth, on the third day of March.
